blocks.name: current_goal (goal projection)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base ProverWorkspaceView, every turn, every profile incl. L1 (it is one of the 5 keys L1 keeps).
blocks.profileGating: ALL profiles. L1 _keep_top_level allow-set includes current_goal (surface_profiles.py:482-490); L2/L3/L4 keep it too.
blocks.trigger: always (the active goal text). syntactic, not proof-need
blocks.consumesFrom: proof_state.goal / proof_ir goal text via _state_panel
blocks.notes: THE primary surface. current_goal.lines is what CLAUDE.md tells the agent to read first. Carries text_fully_shown/truncated + a current.out fallback. No omission/commission concern; this is the load-bearing block.

blocks.name: proof_status
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view every turn; L2+ (NOT in L1 keep-set). Top-level key.
blocks.profileGating: L2/L3/L4 keep it. L1 drops it (L1 keep-set has no proof_status — only schema_version/kind/proof_status? — actually proof_status IS in L1 keep-set at :483). present L1+.
blocks.trigger: always
blocks.consumesFrom: state.status/remaining_goals + phase.name
blocks.notes: Status-correctness guard: a failed/no-progress tactic with remaining_goals>0 is REWRITTEN to status='open' (lines 450-456) so the committed-state panel never contradicts the open goal. Good commission hygiene.

blocks.name: program_frontier
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view; L2+ only (in L2/L3/L4 keep-sets, NOT L1).
blocks.profileGating: l2_semantic_ir/l3/l4 keep_top_level include program_frontier (surface_profiles.py:500,545,588). L1 drops it.
blocks.trigger: present when the goal has a program/frontier alignment (pRHL/equiv). content-gated by frontier presence
blocks.daemonVerified: no (static alignment parse of the rendered goal)
blocks.consumesFrom: frontier panel (resource_focus, frontier_alignment, call_sites, procedure_navigation)
blocks.notes: Heuristic alignment parse. Known commission risk codified in code: _surgery_where (surface_profiles.py:757) had to special-case setup-rows that are actually proc calls (was mislabeling RO set/get as sp/wp-absorbable — equiv_step4 panel-defect #1). first_instruction_alignment is the head the lean phase view leans on.

blocks.name: call_site_surface
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view; in L4_checked_action_surface explicitly re-added (surface_profiles.py:597-608). Also feeds the L4 call_focus phase render.
blocks.profileGating: Surfaces at L4_checked_action_surface (the per-key whitelist at 599 includes call_site_surface). At L2/L3 it rides inside the base panel only if not erased; the top-level call_site_surface key is L4-gated.
blocks.trigger: present when ProofIR exposes a call_site_route with actionable named handles. content-gated
blocks.daemonVerified: partial — handles carry callable_now/requires_cut flags from ProofIR; not all are daemon-probed at view time
blocks.consumesFrom: navigation_context.handles.call_site_route (ec_proof_ir)
blocks.notes: _call_site_handle_is_actionable (:480) DROPS source_lookup_landmark handles as 'dangled noise'. _is_placeholder_handle (surface_profiles.py:1219) drops metavariable symbols (lemma/pair). Good commission filtering. This is the PUSH twin of the pull-only call_site_options inspect topic.

blocks.name: facts_and_diagnostics
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view; L2+ keep-sets. Probability-budget child is L4-only.
blocks.profileGating: L2/L3/L4 keep it (filtered by _filter_facts_and_diagnostics surface_profiles.py:1634). probability_budget child kept ONLY at l4_checked_action_surface (include_probability_budget gate at :612-617). Under view_neutrality_strict, probability_budget is a _HEURISTIC_KEY → stripped.
blocks.trigger: present when frontier/phase resources carry any of ~18 fact keys (missing_live_facts, seq_cut_coverage, preserved_vars, unfoldable_goal_heads, pr_obligation_primary_strategy...). content-gated
blocks.consumesFrom: phase.resources + frontier.resource_focus; probability_budget from analyze_probability_budget(goal_text); gap_signal from unconstrained_post_fields
blocks.notes: COMMISSION: pr_obligation_primary_strategy is a heuristic strategy label that ALSO appears in _HEURISTIC_KEYS — it leaks into facts here under non-strict mode. gap_signal (unconstrained_post_fields) is a genuinely useful, under-advertised fact.

blocks.name: application_context (selected_handles)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view; L2+ keep-sets. Filtered per-profile.
blocks.profileGating: L2/L3/L4 keep it (filtered by _filter_application_context surface_profiles.py:1606; probability-budget handles dropped unless L4). At most 2 options surfaced (:607).
blocks.trigger: present when decision_context has proof options OR a call_invariant_inputs block. content-gated
blocks.daemonVerified: partial — carries each option's confidence/epistemic_status; some are daemon-probe-accepted, most are static
blocks.consumesFrom: _workspace_proof_options(decision_context) OR _call_invariant_inputs (state/frontier/proof_ir/evidence)
blocks.notes: When a call (_: Inv) move is available, this becomes the rich call-invariant context (visible_call_frontier, declaration_requirements, residual_obligations, call_equiv_context). That call-invariant payload is high-value and PUSHED — a positive. Cap of 2 (:607) may DROP a relevant 3rd handle (mild omission).

blocks.name: candidate_moves (moves)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view; L2+ keep-sets, filtered per profile (navigation/structural/route-health/probe-alternatives toggles).
blocks.profileGating: L2: include_navigation/structural=False. L3: nav+structural=True. L4_checked: + upgrade_structural + route_health + demote_partial_commit. _filter_candidate_moves surface_profiles.py:1686.
blocks.trigger: sourced from ProofIR candidate_menu (typed option space); falls back to decision_context proof options when empty. content-gated
blocks.daemonVerified: partial — menu items run through _proof_option_from_action (applicability/template scaffolding); not all daemon-probed
blocks.consumesFrom: proof_ir.candidate_menu (filtered: avoid_action items dropped at :716); else _workspace_proof_options
blocks.notes: COMMISSION-avoidance by design: avoid_action items are NOT surfaced (no anti-route panel, view boundary). NO ranker order/cap — factual option space only. L4 demote_partial_commit_candidates hides accepted-but-residual-surgery openers (a deliberate L4 commission control).

blocks.name: inspect_lookup_handles (ask_manager_for + lookup_candidates) — THE OFFERED MENU
blocks.surfacesWhere: Base view every turn; this IS the agent's tool menu (which inspect topics it is told it can ask for).
blocks.profileGating: Filtered by _filter_inspect_lookup_handles (surface_profiles.py:1663) → only manager-request-allowed handles kept; probe words stripped if probe off. Topic still gated by surface_profile_allows_intent.
blocks.trigger: built from goal_family + goal_type (a SYNTACTIC switch, NOT proof-need). _manager_context_handles maps goal_family∈{probability,relational_program,procedure_frontier,seq_cut,failure_diagnostic,ambient_logic} → a FIXED handle list.
blocks.menuWiring: content_gated_by_goal_family — THIS is the omission lens. The menu is NOT the static profile allow-set; it is the goal_family→handle table at _manager_context_handles.
blocks.consumesFrom: more_context.ask_manager_for + decision_context.context_handles + proof_ir.candidate_menu inspection_action items
blocks.notes: KILLER OMISSION PATTERN (matches the CALL-surface finding): the high-value pull producers reach the agent ONLY if their topic is in the goal_family list here. call_invariant_skeleton/call_site_options are listed for relational_program/procedure_frontier/seq_cut. But pr_bridge_routes/equiv_bridge_lemmas are listed ONLY for goal_family=='probability'; bridge_options/bridge_probe/inv_from_lemma/rewrite_candidates/proof_frontier/subgoal_gap appear in the L4 allow-set (UPGRADED_NAVIGATOR_INSPECT_TOPICS) but are NEVER added to ANY goal_family handle list → profile_allowed_but_never_offered. inv_from_lemma in particular (eval/CLAUDE.md-mandated) is allowed at L4 yet has zero offer path here.

blocks.name: last_result / latest_observation (carries commit-hook text to agent)
blocks.producer: workflow/proof_management/turn_view.py:21 latest_observation_for_view + :49 view_with_latest_observation; content built by workflow/proof_management/backend_actions.py:207 _agent_observation_from_command / :308 _content_observation_from_payload
blocks.surfacesWhere: Prepended to EVERY post-action view as last_result; this is the channel by which commit/probe results AND commit-hook recommendations reach the agent.
blocks.profileGating: last_result is in EVERY profile keep-set incl L1 (surface_profiles.py:485). Not stripped.
blocks.trigger: always after any intent
blocks.daemonVerified: yes (reflects the real EC commit/probe verdict via [TACTIC-EXECUTION-RESULT] marker)
blocks.consumesFrom: the backend stdout [TACTIC-EXECUTION-RESULT] payload: result/execution/error_summary + content from payload.tool_view.guidance.recommendations and payload.observations
blocks.notes: CRITICAL WIRING: commit-hook AUTO-* blocks are appended to the backend `display` text (session_runtime.py:1577-1587) and recorded as diagnostic.emitted events with structured recommendations (1605-1616). The agent-facing observation surfaces STRUCTURED recommendations from payload.tool_view.guidance.recommendations / payload.observations (backend_actions.py:336-345), not the raw marker text. So a commit hook reaches the agent iff its HookResult.recommendations land in the tactic-execution payload.

blocks.name: [POST-CALL-INV-HINT]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline (append_block) on every commit; layer 0. Below the surface-profile boundary (session-runtime).
blocks.profileGating: NONE at the hook layer — fires for all profiles. Reaches the agent via last_result content channel.
blocks.trigger: PROOF-NEED state machine: 3 consecutive errors/no-progress on subgoals spawned by a prior committed `call (_: Inv)` with no fan-out subgoal closed. State in post_call_inv_count.txt.
blocks.consumesFrom: CommitHookContext counts (has_new_error/no_progress/prev_count/curr_count)
blocks.notes: A genuine flailing-detector nudge. Points agent to call <NAMED_EQUIV> / transitivity alternatives. Reasonable; fires rarely (3-strike). Low commission risk.

blocks.name: [GOAL_CLOSED]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0, all profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none (hook layer)
blocks.trigger: prev_count>0 and curr_count>0 and curr_count<prev_count (partial close). syntactic on goal-count delta
blocks.consumesFrom: CommitHookContext prev_count/curr_count
blocks.notes: Pure factual progress signal. No commission concern.

blocks.name: [ALL_GOALS_CLOSED]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0, all profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: no_more (EC 'No more goals') OR async_check_close (qed. → async mode). factual
blocks.consumesFrom: ctx.no_more / ctx.async_check_close
blocks.notes: Factual. Async-check variant correctly warns full-file verify is the source of truth for SMT.

blocks.name: [goal:<type>] (goal header)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 1 (sits above 'State:'); all profiles. Also fired by Session._auto_goal_header on goal-info path.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: always when active_goal present; classifies via ec_goal_parser.classify_goal. syntactic
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_goal_parser.classify_goal/extract_event_vars
blocks.notes: COMMISSION-flavored guidance embedded: eager goals get '⚠ Use eager proc, NOT swap/sim/call' and probability goals with non-res event vars get 'use explicit byequiv postcondition'. These are prescriptive nudges baked into a header — the eager one is a known-correct steer, but it is a hardcoded directive, not a neutral fact. Worth auditing for the eager-blocked-then-actually-works class (see memory: equiv_fwhile).

blocks.name: [STRICT_WARNING]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles. Also a chain-handler variant (inline, not via hooks).
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: no_more AND has_new_error AND every new error line is 'cannot prove goal'+'strict'. factual error-text match
blocks.consumesFrom: diff of raw_curr vs raw_prev error lines
blocks.notes: Has a MUTATION side effect: suppress_error=True flips has_new_error→False so downstream hooks don't treat the strict-only close as failure. Correct guardrail.

blocks.name: [GOAL-TOO-LARGE]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles. Also fired from -goal-info directly.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: active goal > 8000 bytes (is_goal_too_large). syntactic size
blocks.consumesFrom: session_goal_context.is_goal_too_large/too_large_warning_block
blocks.notes: Factual size warning + recovery moves (usually inline* blew up the tree). Useful, low risk.

blocks.name: [TACTIC_NO_EFFECT_AUTO_REVERTED]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: ctx.no_progress (accepted-but-no-progress, detected upstream by Session.detect_no_progress). factual
blocks.consumesFrom: ctx.no_progress; requests request_rollback=True (caller restores history/steps/curr + invalidates daemon)
blocks.notes: MUTATION side effect (auto-rollback). Tells agent NOT to run -prev. Correct and important guardrail against silent no-ops.

blocks.name: [DAEMON_REJECTED]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles. The [CLASS:...] classifier follows inline.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: ctx.daemon_rejection_error non-empty (daemon rejected the tactic: parse error / unknown lemma / cannot infer placeholders). factual
blocks.consumesFrom: EC commit result rejection_error threaded into ctx
blocks.notes: Plugs a silence gap: without it, daemon rejections on tactics AUTO-SIG/AUTO-KB don't match (e.g. `ecall <LEMMA>`) were fully silent. High-value factual surface.

blocks.name: [AUTO-NOPROG-HINT]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles. Fires after TACTIC_NO_EFFECT_AUTO_REVERTED.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: ctx.no_progress AND session_api.explain_no_progress(tactic, raw_prev) returns advice. proof-need diagnostic
blocks.consumesFrom: session_api.explain_no_progress (extracts trailing non-call statements, suggests wp/sp/seq peeling, warns ={glob X} mismatches)
blocks.notes: Compiler-style diagnostic. Generally useful; the ={glob X} warning is a known step4_badi pain point (see memory). Heuristic but evidence-grounded.

blocks.name: [STATE-DIFF]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles (on a successful commit).
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: committed (not no_progress and not has_new_error). always-on for successful commits
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_state_diff.compute_state_diff/format_state_diff_block. SIDE EFFECT: appends verdict|delta|tactic line to commit_meta.log (feeds AUTO-DIFF repeat-warnings)
blocks.notes: Structural verdict (subgoal count, Pr terms, quantifiers, module nesting, connectives) + cosmetic-noise detection (beta/eta/iota/unreduced-glob). Factual. The commit_meta.log write runs even when block text is empty (alignment requirement).

blocks.name: [CLASS:...] (rejection classifier)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline display, after DAEMON_REJECTED; all profiles. Also called from -try/-try-chain/-chain (4 sites).
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: _rejection_for_display non-empty (a rejected tactic). factual
blocks.consumesFrom: _classify_and_format(rejection, tactic_text, file_path)
blocks.notes: Lives inline (not a registry hook) because shared across 4 call sites. Pairs with DAEMON_REJECTED. Factual classification of why a tactic failed — high value, kept out of the registry only for code-locality reasons.

blocks.name: [AUTO-PIVOT] / [AUTO-PIVOT-VERIFIED] (commit-time)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 3 (unverified) — ONLY the cheap static-scored variant fires on commit. All profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none at hook layer; but the recommendations carry epistemic_status='legacy_kb_hint_not_frontier_verified' for unverified entries.
blocks.trigger: active_goal matches a pre-declared catalog lemma shape (pivot_applicability scoring). content-gated by catalog + per-shape dedup (_seen_shapes)
blocks.daemonVerified: no on the commit path — commit-time PivotStrategyPhase.run emits ONLY static-scored pivots (the daemon verification moved to inspect mode='verified', pull-only). partial: it claims DIRECT/ARG_DIFF apply tactics from a pure shape match.
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_pr_path_diff.pivot_applicability over catalog from context_file
blocks.notes: DELIBERATE PUSH→PULL MIGRATION (commission control): the heavy daemon-verified pivot probes used to block every `proc.` commit for tens of seconds; run() now emits only cheap static context and the verified/call-ready/bridge/rewrite probes are inspect-only. So on commit the agent gets UNVERIFIED pivot shape-matches (a guess); the VERIFIED twin requires pulling pivot-inspect. This is the same omission shape as the CALL finding — the best (daemon-verified) content is pull-only.

blocks.name: [AUTO-PIVOT-CALL-READY] (commit-time: NOT emitted)
blocks.surfacesWhere: NOT on the commit path anymore — only via inspect (call_site_options topic).
blocks.profileGating: reachable only through the call_site_options / pivot inspect route (L4 UPGRADED_NAVIGATOR set, or the relational_program goal_family handle).
blocks.trigger: daemon accepts `call <name>.` for an equiv pivot on the current goal. daemon-need
blocks.menuWiring: profile_allowed_but_never_offered as a PUSH (it is pull-only now)
blocks.consumesFrom: _probe_call_ready (daemon try_tactic over equiv pivots)
blocks.notes: OMISSION: this is daemon-verified `call NAME.` readiness — exactly the high-value CALL content — and it NO LONGER pushes on commit (run() drops it). The commit-time AUTO-DIFF used to annotate diff lines with ✓daemon-ready from this; now scratch['pivot.call_ready_names'] is empty on commit so AUTO-DIFF call lines stay UNANNOTATED. Documented in PivotStrategyPhase docstring (lines 274-279).

blocks.name: [AUTO-CALL-SUGGEST] / [CALL-SITE-OPTIONS/*] (commit-time)
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 3; all profiles. Runs with h=None (no daemon) on commit.
blocks.profileGating: none at hook layer
blocks.trigger: is_prhl AND not pr-eq-only. content-gated
blocks.daemonVerified: no on commit (called with daemon=None at :387) → emits CALL-SITE-OPTIONS/NO_ROUTE or /CONTEXT_ONLY or /UNAVAILABLE, never the VERIFIED block
blocks.menuWiring: always_on (commit) but degenerate — only the no-route/unavailable variants reach commit
blocks.consumesFrom: _proof_ir_call_site_route (ProofIR call_site_route handle)
blocks.notes: On commit it gets daemon=None so it can only surface the NEGATIVE variants (no verified call). The VERIFIED call-site options (the useful payload) require inspect mode='call_site'. Another push-stub / pull-real split.

blocks.name: [AUTO-SELF-HINTS/LEGACY_NARRATIVE_REJECTED]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 4, once per session; all profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: target lemma has legacy narrative proof-hint fields (closer_hints/call_template/invariant_sketch...). once-per-session
blocks.consumesFrom: session._load_narrative lemma_catalog match on target lemma
blocks.notes: COMMISSION (benign): this block exists ONLY to announce that legacy narrative hints were REJECTED/not shown (eval-clean discipline). It is a transparency note that the agent gets nothing here — arguably noise on layer 4. Pure negative-space marker.

blocks.name: [AUTO-DIFF]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 3; all profiles. One emit per alignment shape (_seen_alignment_shapes dedup).
blocks.profileGating: none at hook layer; recommendations stamped with epistemic_status (e.g. static_call_alignment_not_frontier_verified).
blocks.trigger: active_goal is pRHL/equiv with stmts (program_alignment_diff), or probability (probability_diff), or eager (eager_diff). content-gated
blocks.daemonVerified: no by default — call_ready annotation only when PivotStrategyPhase wrote scratch['pivot.call_ready_names'] AND daemon reachable; on commit that scratch is now EMPTY (see AUTO-PIVOT-CALL-READY), so AUTO-DIFF is UNANNOTATED.
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_pr_path_diff (program_alignment_diff/probability_diff/eager_diff) + _match_call_equivs (local equivs from context_file)
blocks.notes: Structural program/Pr/eager diff with extracted tactic recommendations (call/swap/seq/wp/sp/...). USEFUL but the call-readiness ✓/⚠ annotation is now dead on commit (its data source moved to pull-only). So AUTO-DIFF surfaces `call NAME` suggestions WITHOUT the daemon-ready check it was designed to carry — a degraded-since-refactor commission risk.

blocks.name: HintDispatchPhase: [AUTO-ABSTRACT-ADV-CALL]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 2; all profiles. Per-(producer,goal-prefix) dedup.
blocks.profileGating: none at hook layer
blocks.trigger: goal_type=='pRHL' AND ec_abstract_adv_hint.detect_and_propose finds abstract-adversary call sites + canonical-Inv call tactics. content-gated, daemon-verified
blocks.daemonVerified: yes — dispatch_hints Layer-2 daemon-probes each candidate (probe_budget 6/generator); accepted→runnable_tactic
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_abstract_adv_hint.detect_and_propose over context_file source
blocks.notes: HIGH-VALUE PUSHED + DAEMON-VERIFIED: covers `call (_: canonical Inv)` for abstract `declare module A` adversaries that the NAMED-equiv producers can't reach (no equiv lemma). This is one of the few genuinely daemon-verified commit-time pushes. Positive.

blocks.name: HintDispatchPhase: [AUTO-SWAP-ALIGN]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 2; all profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: goal_type=='pRHL' AND swap_align.parse_prhl_goal finds swap candidates. content-gated, daemon-verified
blocks.daemonVerified: yes (Layer-2 daemon probe of each swap{N} K M.)
blocks.consumesFrom: analysis.swap_align.parse_prhl_goal over context_file
blocks.notes: = `-align` fired automatically post-commit, daemon-verified. The agent no longer needs to invoke -align manually. Genuinely useful PUSH; daemon-checked.

blocks.name: HintDispatchPhase: [AUTO-ASYM-SEQ]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 2; all profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: goal_type=='pRHL' AND ec_asym_seq_hint.detect_and_propose_all finds asymmetric (unequal stmt count) split points with a synthesized invariant. content-gated, daemon-verified
blocks.daemonVerified: yes (synthesized seq N M : inv. daemon-probed; rejected→strategy_hint fallback)
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_asym_seq_hint.detect_and_propose_all + swap_align column matches for the invariant
blocks.notes: Synthesizes a CONCRETE seq invariant (vs AUTO-DIFF's <inv> placeholder) and daemon-checks it. Even rejected candidates surface as strategy_hint with the concrete shape. Strong positive — turns a placeholder into an actionable starting invariant.

blocks.name: [AUTO-SIG]
blocks.surfacesWhere: Commit pipeline, layer 0; all profiles. Once per (lemma,session) via _seen_names.
blocks.profileGating: none at hook layer
blocks.trigger: has_new_error AND a failing apply/exact/rewrite/have/byequiv/ecall/call references a lemma whose signature can be looked up (name from EC error text priority 1, tactic text priority 2). proof-need on failure
blocks.daemonVerified: no (signature lookup, not a probe) — but the SIGNATURE is ground truth from source
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_search.lookup_lemma_signature over include dirs + source sibling + easycrypt-src/theories
blocks.notes: HIGH-VALUE: on a failed lemma application it fetches the EXACT signature so the agent stops guessing arity. Once-per-lemma dedup. The lemma-lookup-discipline contract (CLAUDE.md) made automatic. Strong positive, low commission.

blocks.name: [SEARCH-QUERY-AUTOFIX]
blocks.surfacesWhere: -search handler ONLY (run_search_hooks), prepended above search results. All profiles (search is below profile boundary).
blocks.profileGating: none — but only reachable when the agent runs a search (via lemma_hints/lemma_index inspect or backend -search).
blocks.trigger: raw_query contained BRE `\|` alternation → auto-rewrote to ERE `|` and re-ran. syntactic
blocks.consumesFrom: autofix_bre_alternation on the raw query
blocks.notes: Factual correction notice (shows original vs fixed + hit count). Prevents a silent 0-hit from BRE/ERE confusion. Useful, low risk.

blocks.name: [SEARCH-FALLBACK-HINT]
blocks.surfacesWhere: -search handler ONLY; after 3 consecutive 0-hit searches. All profiles.
blocks.profileGating: none
blocks.trigger: 3 consecutive 'No matches for' searches (state in search_no_hit_count.txt). proof-need
blocks.consumesFrom: the search output no-hit detection (canonical no-hit counter)
blocks.notes: OMISSION-FIXER: after repeated name-regex misses, points the agent at -search-skeleton (EC native AST op-AND search) and -lemma-hints (op-indexed) — tools the agent likely doesn't know. Good. But it ONLY fires inside the search path: if the agent never reaches for search (e.g. because the goal_family menu didn't offer lemma_hints/lemma_index), this never triggers.

blocks.name: goal_info
blocks.surfacesWhere: every turn; manager forwards inspect_context -> session backend
blocks.profileGating: ALL profiles allowing inspect_context: BASIC/SIGNPOST/PREVIEW/UPGRADED (surface_profiles.py:57-105). Always allowed where inspect_context is.
blocks.trigger: agent asks; offered always
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_goal_parser (parsed goal structure + resolved names)

blocks.name: diagnose
blocks.producer: core/easycrypt/commands/inspect_commands.py:handle_diagnose (826); intercepted at manager: proof_node_manager.py:636 _handle_inspect_diagnose (probe-aware). CLI -diagnose via _inspect_args:954
blocks.surfacesWhere: failure_diagnostic goal-family turns; manager-intercepted
blocks.profileGating: BASIC/SIGNPOST/PREVIEW/UPGRADED (all non-empty allow-sets)
blocks.trigger: after a tactic/probe failed (error to classify)
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_error_classifier (factual error attribution) + recovery diagnosis
blocks.notes: Offered as a handle ONLY for goal_family==failure_diagnostic (session_prover_workspace_view.py:2326-2331, 2558-2563). Manager intercepts before EC backend to make it probe-aware. daemonVerified=partial: classifies last error, no re-verify of a fix.

blocks.name: tactic_forms
blocks.surfacesWhere: relational_program/procedure_frontier/seq_cut/probability/ambient_logic turns (pre-seeded name=call/sp/wp/swap/while/rewrite)
blocks.profileGating: BASIC/SIGNPOST/PREVIEW/UPGRADED (all non-empty allow-sets)
blocks.trigger: agent asks; offered (pre-filled name) on most program/pr goal-families
blocks.menuWiring: content_gated_by_goal_family (multiple families emit pre-filled tactic_forms handles)
blocks.consumesFrom: ec_tactic_forms static form table (no daemon)
blocks.notes: Heavily offered: _manager_context_handles emits several tactic_forms handles per family with payload name pre-set. Static table — NOT daemon-checked against the goal.

blocks.name: align
blocks.surfacesWhere: relational_program / seq_cut turns
blocks.profileGating: SIGNPOST/PREVIEW/UPGRADED (L2-L4). NOT BASIC; NOT L1.
blocks.trigger: agent asks; offered when LHS/RHS order may need swap/alignment
blocks.consumesFrom: frontier alignment (program_frontier)
blocks.notes: Emitted as handle for relational_program (2451) and seq_cut (2539).

blocks.name: call_subgoals
blocks.surfacesWhere: relational_program/procedure_frontier/seq_cut turns; needs invariant payload

A frontier mis-annotation silently mis-targets every downstream call panel.

Control-suffix-legality + obligation-stack give phase-legality inputs.

Only 1 non-test importer (procedure_frontend) — so if the procedure menu is profile-gated off, the sampling/coupling analysis is invisible.

blocks.name: ec_name_resolution

Emits NameResolutionFact (exact-signature status, lookup action, declaration slots). Does NOT run EC or decide tactics. Pairs with ec_instantiation_binding (slots) to make a handle usable. The name-status badge is the per-item content that tells the agent 'inspect this lemma before applying'.

blocks.name: ec_instantiation_binding

blocks.notes: Searches source/context/goal for conservative slot-fill values; candidates carry evidence + confidence but the pass NEVER verifies or commits (read-only). 5 non-test importers. `_instantiation_missing_slot_labels` surfaces which slots are still UNFILLED — a useful 'what's missing' signal for the agent. Complements name_resolution: name says 'this declares X', binding says 'X's module slot could be M1/M2'.

blocks.name: ec_native_ast_search

blocks.notes: EC-native search FRONTEND — proposes conservative -search-skeleton queries and consumes prior hits as typed -where inspection candidates, but does NOT run EC (the backend in ../search/ runs it). A hit becomes a READ-ONLY -where inspection action until signature/instantiation are known (never auto-apply). 2 non-test importers. The typed-fact bridge between 'goal shape' and 'go look up this lemma'.

blocks.name: ec_dataflow_invariant
blocks.surfacesWhere: ec_proof_ir spine via ec_proof_ir_handles.py:57 → invariant_skeleton handle, used by ec_proof_action_surface._while_invariant_tactic_hint (the `while (<inv>)` / `call (_: Inv)` hint)

blocks.notes: Lightweight dataflow: starts from post-state vars, walks statements BACKWARD, surfaces the equality resources an invariant probably needs. Explicitly 'conservative and diagnostic' — does NOT replace EC's typed variable-access analysis and does NOT daemon-check the suggested invariant. There IS a noise guard (`_invariant_looks_noisy` in ec_proof_action_surface gates whether the suggested_invariant text is shown vs a placeholder). 3 non-test importers. COMMISSION risk class: a backward-walk invariant guess is a strategy hint that may be wrong; the noise-guard + placeholder fallback is the mitigation.

blocks.name: ec_goal_parser

Classifies goal family + extracts shallow syntactic fields. 3 non-test importers but EVERYTHING downstream depends on its parse — a classification miss caps the entire producer chain for that goal. Not agent-offered directly; it is the substrate.

blocks.notes: Small but load-bearing: the EC_NATIVE_AUTHORITY_RANK=100 vs PRETTY_TEXT=10 arbiter that enforces the CONTRACTS ground-truth rule (native artifact outranks regex for same name/statement). 4 non-test importers. Not a panel producer — it is the trust layer that prevents a regex interpretation from masquerading as EC fact.

This is the central COMMISSION GUARD — it is what prevents a 'looks-applicable' guess from being labeled runnable. Not content; it is the safety typing on content.

Ordering is structural build order; the only factual re-order is the Pr-bridge chain (_order_instantiated_pr_bridges in ec_proof_action_surface). KEY menuWiring fact: the menu is the union of content-gated items — a producer that computes nothing for the current goal contributes nothing, which is exactly the content-gate mechanism that made bridge_lemmas/inv_from_lemma 0-offered on the SEPARATE inspect path.

Internal — surfaces only through the facade.

This is a SECOND content-gate INSIDE the menu spine (distinct from the workspace-view content-gate): a candidate can be silently dropped here, an omission surface to audit.

Mechanism, not panel.

blocks.name: ec_proof_diagnostics

This is the 'why did my tactic fail, in structural terms' surface. Pairs with ec_error_classifier (which does the coarse SYNTAX-vs-STRUCTURE attribution this builds on).

blocks.name: ec_error_classifier
blocks.surfacesWhere: session_common.py + session_runtime.py (runtime, on every tactic error) + workflow/proof_management/backend_actions.py; the SYNTAX/STRUCTURE classification rides on the rejection result the agent sees
blocks.profileGating: the error classification is attached at the runtime/backend boundary — reaches the agent on essentially any profile that surfaces tactic failures
blocks.trigger: proof-need: a raw EC error string to attribute (SYNTAX = name/structure accepted, only surface-syntax wrong; STRUCTURE = goal/program-shape assumption wrong)
blocks.menuWiring: content_gated_by_ec_error_classifier (an error occurred) — but wired at runtime so effectively always_on for failures
blocks.consumesFrom: raw EC error string only
blocks.notes: Factual attribution of the error KIND (not what to do next). Motivated by ChaChaPoly step1: right plan, wrong syntax (.distinguish(tt) vs .distinguish()) — SYNTAX class tells the agent 'tweak the form, don't abandon the route', the highest-leverage steering a failure can carry. 4 non-test importers, wired at the runtime/backend boundary (closer to a commit-hook than a menu producer). Feeds ec_proof_diagnostics.

blocks.name: ec_state_diff
blocks.surfacesWhere: session_hooks.py (the _SEARCH/_COMMIT hook registry) — emits a [STATE-DIFF] block after a tactic, classifying the pre->post goal transition; reaches the agent as post-tactic transition feedback
blocks.profileGating: [STATE-DIFF] block surfaces on profiles that include the state-diff hook output (the diff-bearing surface)
blocks.trigger: proof-need: a tactic just executed — compares pre vs post goal text (subgoal count, Pr terms, quantifiers, module-nesting depth, connectives, cosmetic-noise patterns)
blocks.daemonVerified: n/a (it observes the ACTUAL committed transition — ground truth by construction)
blocks.menuWiring: content_gated_by_ec_state_diff (a transition occurred) — wired via session_hooks
blocks.consumesFrom: pre_raw + post_raw goal text + the tactic (also feeds ec_pr_path_diff)
blocks.notes: Pre-vs-post-tactic TIME diff (distinct axis from ec_pr_path_diff's LHS-vs-RHS module-tree diff). Classifies the transition + flags cosmetic noise (beta-redex, unreduced glob chains, eta wrappers, iota match-redex) so the agent isn't misled by a no-op-looking rewrite. 2 non-test importers (session_hooks + ec_pr_path_diff). Because it observes the committed result it is the one 'diff' producer whose content is intrinsically ground-truth.

blocks.name: probability_budget
blocks.surfacesWhere: session_prover_workspace_view.py imports it DIRECTLY (route context in the view) + workflow/surface_profiles.py + workflow/progress.py + analyzers/route_health.py + proof_node_resume.py

blocks.consumesFrom: goal text (shape detection); probability_budget_route_risk additionally reads recent ACCEPTED/probed tactic status (the 'accepted/probed' strings are CONSUMED history, NOT its own verification)
blocks.notes: 75KB static route-context analyzer — does NOT propose a proof script, only surfaces route context + route-health risk (e.g. 'an accepted seq cut may allocate the budget to the wrong branch'; 'do not treat an accepted multi-rnd probe as route quality'). Directly wired into the workspace view (rare) so it reaches the agent broadly. Its high daemon/probe grep count is reading prior-tactic acceptance, not verifying — daemonVerified=no. 4 non-test importers. COMMISSION watch: it explicitly warns against MIS-reading accepted probes as progress — i.e. it is partly a counter-commission lamp.

blocks.consumesFrom: the fully-built ProverWorkspaceView
blocks.notes: NOT a content producer — it is the NON-FATAL runtime invariant checker (the defense-in-depth half of the 'close the finite panel-bug space' plan; generative half = tests/test_panel_properties.py). Reports VIOLATIONS rather than raising, so a NEW goal shape that breaks a panel invariant is caught in the audit log instead of silently reaching the agent. 2 non-test importers. Listed for completeness: it is QA infrastructure ABOUT the panels, not a panel.

blocks.name: analyzers/call_site
blocks.surfacesWhere: AnalyzerPipeline → consumed by session_prover_workspace_view.py + session_workspace_view_manager.py + panel_policy.py + surface_profiles.py + repl_session.py → call-site evidence/surface in the view's decision_context

blocks.notes: 34KB — the call-site EVIDENCE analyzer (a SEPARATE surfacing path from ec_proof_ir: analyzers feed the view via AnalyzerPipeline + panel_policy, not the candidate_menu). Pure evidence: does not mutate proof state or run tactics. This is the workflow-side counterpart to the ec_* call-family producers — overlap/duplication risk between this and ec_call_frontier_structure/ec_oracle_diff (both describe the call frontier from different layers). Heavily consumed (the pipeline is wired into the view + manager + profiles).

This is the seq-cut analog to ec_asym_seq_hint but on the analyzer/evidence path rather than the hook-phase path — another cross-layer overlap (two producers about seq decomposition).

Pure evidence pass. looks_like_pure_tail_goal is ALSO imported by analyzers/call_site (the two coordinate: call-site evidence vs pure-tail evidence are complementary route classifiers). No daemon.

blocks.name: analyzers/route_health
blocks.surfacesWhere: AnalyzerPipeline → workspace-view route-health + structural-transition evidence; consumed by surface_profiles.py + repl_session.py + recovery.py

Deliberately pure (reads aggregated node state, does not mutate/render). 7 non-test importers — the most-wired analyzer. Consumes probability_budget for product-bound route risk. This is the 'is my current route healthy / am I making structural progress' lamp — the main give-up/keep-going evidence surface (per the heuristic-cleanup memory, the give-up gate became a single gentle nudge here).

Ensures the surfaced route-health is consistent with the recovery diagnosis (workspace_view_with_recovery_consistent_route_health) — avoids a contradictory 'route healthy but recover anyway' panel. 1 non-test analyzer importer + recovery.py. Evidence/policy, no daemon.

Complements ec_concrete_global_frame (which builds the concrete `={...}` frame at a call) — frame_obligation is the cross-scope ledger, ec_concrete_global_frame is the per-call extraction; another cross-layer pairing on the 'frame' theme.

blocks.name: analyzers/pipeline
blocks.surfacesWhere: the container that runs FrameObligationAnalyzer (+ the route/call-site/seq-cut/pure-tail/route-health/recovery analyzers) and returns their evidence to the workspace view / manager
blocks.profileGating: n/a (orchestrator) — individual analyzer outputs are profile-gated by panel_policy downstream
blocks.trigger: always-on when the analyzer evidence path runs
blocks.menuWiring: always_on (container) — each analyzer's evidence is content_gated_by panel_policy
blocks.consumesFrom: the individual analyzers
blocks.notes: The analyzer-side analog of ec_proof_ir: the orchestration boundary for the workflow/proof_management EVIDENCE path (distinct from the ec_proof_ir candidate_menu path). This is the second of the TWO surfacing spines in scope — ec_proof_ir (typed menu) vs AnalyzerPipeline (route evidence). Knowing which spine a producer feeds is the key to its menuWiring.
